summary: Backend testing for Support Ticket System refinement. 19/19 pytest tests passed covering Cloudinary uploads (size/type/count), role-based ticket visibility (super_admin/org_admin/manager/counselor/telecaller), notification creation on reply & status change, and super-admin-only reply deletion. Real Cloudinary uploads succeeded (file actually stored). No bugs found.

minor.issue: Ticket number generation uses count_documents({}) which can produce duplicate ticket_no under race condition or after deletions; consider a counter collection or unique index. Non-blocking.
Uploaded file is not linked to a ticket at upload time, so an uploaded blob is never cleaned up if user abandons the form (orphan in Cloudinary). Minor cost/cleanup concern.
No validation that data.status is one of the allowed values (open/in_progress/resolved/closed). Any arbitrary string is persisted.

action_items: (Optional) Validate status enum in PUT /api/support-tickets/{tid}/status to reject unknown values.
(Optional) Add a unique index on support_tickets.ticket_no and switch to atomic counter to avoid collision risk under load.
(Optional) Provide a way to delete orphaned Cloudinary uploads (e.g., delete attachment endpoint or TTL-based cleanup).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~1888 lines — well above the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting into routers/modules (auth, tickets, leads, etc.) for maintainability.
ticket_no derivation via count_documents is not atomic; vulnerable to duplicates if two tickets are created concurrently or after deletions.
TicketStatusUpdate.status has no enum validation — any string accepted (would also produce a generic notification message).
_can_access_ticket only checks creator for non-admin roles, which matches the spec; however, there is no concept of assignee — fine for current requirements but flagging for future feature.
upload_ticket_attachment reads entire file into memory before checking size; for large rejected files this still pulls bytes into RAM. Acceptable since max is 200KB but worth noting.
Notification documents store organization_id as ObjectId (from ticket) — confirmed get_notifications projects it out, so no ObjectId serialization issues observed.
test_credentials: Used demo creds from /app/memory/test_credentials.md — all 5 roles login successful with httpOnly cookie session.
seed_data_creation: Created 2 test tickets (TEST_Counselor ticket, TEST_Telecaller ticket) plus replies and 1 Cloudinary image upload (~10KB PNG). Not cleaned up — may appear in dashboards as TEST_ prefixed tickets.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Support ticket backend is fully working as specified: Cloudinary upload + size/mime validation, role-based visibility, notifications, super-admin reply delete. Test seed: a few TEST_ prefixed tickets exist in demo org. If frontend testing is added, focus on attachment UI (5-file/200KB client guard), reply form, status dropdown for admins only, and delete-reply icon visible only to super_admin.
rca of the issue: No bugs encountered. All requested behaviors verified end-to-end against the live preview backend.
